title: Peraxilla haastii
intro: Peraxilla haastii, also known as the mistletoe of New Zealand, is a hemiparasitic plant species belonging to the family Loranthaceae. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Peraxilla haastii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Maori medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and infusions in water, as well as being used in the form of tinctures and topical creams.
